text.title: spatial-interaction-patterns Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for spatial-interaction-patterns. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Design at minimum two input modalities for every interaction: a primary modality and a fallback. Gaze+dwell serves as the universal accessibility fallback for users with motor impairments who cannot perform hand gestures.
rule.rationale: Single-modality XR interfaces exclude users whose primary modality is unavailable (gloved hands, controller battery, arm fatigue); a fallback is a functional accessibility requirement.

rule.statement: Use direct manipulation (grab, pinch-resize, two-hand rotate, throw) for objects within arm's reach in the interaction zone. Provide physics-based feedback — resistance, snap-to-grid, collision — to make virtual objects feel real.
rule.rationale: Direct manipulation maps to existing physical world intuitions and requires no learned grammar; it is the most learnable interaction pattern for new XR users.

rule.statement: Use ray-casting (laser pointer from hand or controller) for selecting objects at distance, activating menus, and interacting with wall-mounted or ceiling-mounted content. Require an explicit confirmation action (click, pinch) to prevent accidental activation.
rule.rationale: Ray-casting extends the interaction range beyond arm's reach without requiring physical movement; it is the standard far-field interaction pattern across platforms.

rule.statement: Use gaze+dwell (look at target for 1-2 seconds to activate) as the accessibility fallback for any interaction that normally uses hand or controller input. Display a visible countdown indicator during the dwell period.
rule.rationale: Gaze+dwell enables full XR interaction for users with motor impairments who cannot perform hand gestures or hold controllers; the countdown indicator confirms intent and prevents surprise activations.

rule.statement: Set dwell time to a minimum of 1 second for destructive actions and 0.5 seconds for safe, reversible selections. Make dwell time configurable in accessibility settings.
rule.rationale: Fixed dwell times that are too short cause accidental activations (Midas touch); too long causes fatigue; individual variability requires a configurable range.
